title: Light Flaky Jamaican Pork Patties
description: Savory and flaky Jamaican pork patties featuring allspice, perfect for a hangover cure or a snack. This diabetic-friendly dish is made with a brine-wet method and served on a lazy-susan-spinner.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine the ground pork, allspice, onion, scallion, garlic, salt, black pepper, and paprika.
Mix well with your hands or a wooden spoon until just combined.
Roll out the puff pastry on a floured surface to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Spoon the pork mixture onto one half of the pastry, leaving a 1/2 inch border around the edges.
Brush the edges with a little water, then fold the other half of the pastry over the filling.
Press the edges to seal and use a fork to crimp.
Place the patties on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and brush with a little water.
Bake for 25-30 minutes, or until golden brown.
